headlines.title: Three weeks in, Iran war escalates beyond Trump's control
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump ends the third week of the Iran war confronting a crisis that seems to be slipping out of his hands. Global energy prices are surging, the United States stands isolated from allies, and more troops are preparing to deploy despite Trump's promise the war would be only a 'short excursion.' Iran is choking off Gulf oil and gas supplies while launching missile strikes.

headlines.title: Iran fires long-range missiles at Diego Garcia as Trump considers 'winding down' war
headlines.summary: Israel confirmed Iranian forces fired long-range ballistic missiles for the first time in the war, expanding the risk of attacks beyond the Middle East. Iran launched two 4,000-kilometer-range missiles at the U.S.-UK military base Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean. Trump stated Washington was considering 'winding down' its military operation against Iran.

headlines.title: Dozens injured in Israel after Iranian missile strikes target areas near nuclear research center
headlines.summary: Iran struck two communities near Israel's main nuclear research center late Saturday, leaving buildings shattered and at least seven people seriously injured. It was the first time Israel's nuclear research center has been targeted in the fighting. Israel's military said it was not able to intercept missiles that hit the southern cities of Dimona and Arad.

headlines.title: Iran says US and Israel attacked Natanz nuclear facility
headlines.summary: The United States and Israel have struck Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, according to Tehran's atomic energy organisation. The organisation stated that following the 'criminal attacks,' the Natanz enrichment complex was targeted. No leakage of radioactive materials was reported in the area in central Iran.

headlines.title: G7 ready to act to protect global energy supplies, backs Hormuz Strait security
headlines.summary: The foreign ministers of the Group of Seven countries said on Saturday they stood ready to take necessary measures to support global energy supplies and reaffirmed the importance of safeguarding maritime routes, including in the Strait of Hormuz. The statement comes as oil prices have risen 50% due to the war, hobbling the global economy.

headlines.title: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel in Trump-Russia probe, dies at 81
headlines.summary: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director who led the historic probe into alleged collusion between Donald Trump's 2016 campaign and the Russian government, has died at age 81. Mueller served as FBI director from 2001 to 2013 and was appointed special counsel in 2017 to investigate Russian interference in the 2016 election.

headlines.title: China calls for end to war in Middle East, warns of economic impact
headlines.summary: China called for an end to the war in the Middle East, warning of the impact on global energy, shipping and trade. Beijing stated that 'armed conflict will only breed new hatred' and called for a ceasefire amid rising energy cost concerns. The Chinese foreign minister said 'unjust war should not go on.'

headlines.title: Four killed in Russian drone attacks on Ukraine as thousands left without power
headlines.summary: At least four people have died in Russian drone attacks on Saturday, while strikes on energy infrastructure knocked out power to most of the northern Ukrainian region of Chernihiv. A drone attack in Zaporizhzhia killed two people and injured six, including two children. The attacks continue amid ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the four-year conflict.

Iran is choking off Gulf oil and gas supplies while launching missile strikes.
